a:r.a:t: This flow demonstrates the research_coordinator, which is distinct from the regular coordinator. The user uploads two contracts and asks to compare liability clauses and flag risks. The router detects attachments plus a coordinator campaign intent pattern and routes to research_coordinator. The key difference from the regular coordinator is the worker pool: research_coordinator includes rag_researcher in its allowed_worker_agents list alongside planner, rag_worker, general, graph_manager, finalizer, and verifier. This means it can dispatch the specialized rag_researcher agent for fine-grained corpus exploration. The research planner decomposes the task into parallel document extraction jobs. Two rag_worker instances run simultaneously -- one extracting liability clauses from Contract A, the other from Contract B. Each worker gets a scoped copy of the parent SessionState via _build_scoped_worker_state(), which means workers cannot pollute each other's session data. This is critical for parallel execution -- without session scoping, two workers writing to the same session would create race conditions. The handoff artifact system passes typed intermediate results between pipeline stages. When the rag_workers finish, they produce doc_digest and clause_redline_inventory artifacts. These artifacts have a whitelist controlling which downstream agents can consume them -- the finalizer and general agent can read clause inventories, but a utility agent cannot. The general agent then cross-references the extracted clauses, comparing indemnification limits, force majeure definitions, termination rights, and liability caps between the two contracts. The finalizer synthesizes a structured comparison report. The verifier then performs coverage checking: did we find all liability-related sections? Are the cited clause numbers actually in the source documents? If the verifier finds gaps -- say a limitation of liability section was mentioned in Contract B's table of contents but never extracted -- it triggers a revision loop. The planner receives the verifier's feedback and creates a targeted follow-up task. This loop can run up to 4 rounds, configurable via max_revision_rounds. The result is a comprehensive comparison with clause-level citations and a risk assessment, all verified against the source documents.
